questions.prompt: Complete: "My sister ___ her homework straight after dinner."
questions.explanation: This is a habit, so we use the simple present. My sister = she, and the third person of "do" is "does": she does her homework. "Do" forgets the ending, and "dos" and "doies" are not correct spellings.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We ___ pizza at the new restaurant last Friday."
questions.explanation: "Last Friday" is a finished past time. "Eat" is irregular, and its simple past is "ate": we ate pizza. "Eated" wrongly adds -ed to an irregular verb, "eat" is the present, and "eaten" is the past participle.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right adverb: "Rania is a very serious student, so she ___ forgets her books."
questions.explanation: A serious student almost never forgets her books, so the adverb is "rarely" (≈ 10%). "Always", "usually" and "often" all mean it happens most or many times, which would make her a forgetful student — the opposite of serious.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct question for the answer: "At four o'clock. That's when the match started."

options.text: What time did the match start?

questions.explanation: The answer gives a clock time (four o'clock), so the question asks "What time": What time did the match start? "Where" asks about a place, "Who" about a person, and "Why" about a reason.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Nour was very tired, so she ___ to the party."
questions.explanation: The sentence is past and negative, so we use "didn't" + the base verb: didn't go. "Didn't went" keeps the past form after "did" (wrong), "don't go" is present, and "wasn't go" mixes "to be" with another verb.

questions.prompt: Read the signal word and complete: "Two years ago, my family ___ from Gafsa to Sfax."
questions.explanation: "Ago" always points to a finished past time, so we use the simple past. "Move" is regular, so we add -d (it already ends in e): moved. "Move/moves" are present, and "moving" cannot stand alone.
